title: Ctenopteris thwaitesii
taxonomy: Ctenopteris thwaitesii is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a vascular plant belonging to the division Pteridophyta. The genus Ctenopteris is part of the order Polypodiales. Ctenopteris thwaitesii is native to Sri Lanka. Its classification is a key aspect of understanding its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.
uses: Ctenopteris thwaitesii has been traditionally used in medicine in Sri Lanka. Its leaves are used in the treatment of fever, and are also believed to have anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is said to be effective in treating various other ailments such as skin conditions and respiratory issues. In addition, its leaves are used to make a decoction that is believed to be beneficial for digestive health. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Ctenopteris thwaitesii in modern medicine.
